response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned senior developer with strong polyglot experience, a relevant GIS/geospatial background through GISCollective, and exposure to high-throughput, low-latency systems at Sociomantic (DLang, async I/O, caching). However, the role explicitly requires modern C++ or Rust, and the resume shows only brief, dated C/C++ exposure at National Instruments (2014-2015) and no Rust. Recent work is heavily TypeScript/Node-focused, which is a significant mismatch for a C++ routing-tiling pipeline role. Worth a screen given the geospatial pedigree and systems-programming aptitude in D, but technical depth in C++/Rust must be validated.
strengths: Direct geospatial/GIS experience via founding GISCollective and Green Map work
Low-latency, high-throughput backend experience at Sociomantic with async I/O and fibers
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, testability focus
concerns: No demonstrated modern C++ experience; C/C++ usage is brief and over a decade old
Recent roles are web/full-stack (TS/Node), not systems-level data pipeline work
